description: Declarative catalog of Alp SDK project templates (app archetypes), for a frontend (alp-sdk-vscode, Alp Studio, a future `alp new`) to discover what starter projects exist and what each needs, instead of ad-hoc example copying. Every record maps to a CANONICAL existing example under examples/ -- the template does not fork or duplicate the example, it IS the example, described machine-readably. scripts/check_template_catalog.py validates this file against metadata/schemas/template-catalog-v1.schema.json AND drift-checks every referenced example/testcase.yaml/library/chip against the real tree, so a record can never point at a path or dependency that doesn't exist. Per-record `status` is grounded in the tracked example content-quality gaps in issues #448 (iot-connected-camera documented as v1.0-ready while still a runtime skeleton) and #520 (six display/LVGL + iot-dashboard examples bypass <alp/display.h>/<alp/gui.h> for direct zephyr/drivers/display.h) -- scripts/check_template_catalog.py hard-fails a `stable` record whose `example` is one of those; none of the six templates below reference one. This is the catalog + schema + drift-validation FOUNDATION (epic #610 SS3) only. The deterministic render/preview API -- `alp generate <template-id> <dest>` materialising a template into a fresh directory and running its native_sim test there -- is a deliberately-scoped FOLLOW-UP, tracked separately; it is not built by this change.

templates.title: Minimal -- Hello World
templates.description: The simplest possible Alp SDK app: no peripherals, no chips, no board-specific wiring. Confirms the toolchain, flash flow, and log console are wired correctly before a customer chases harder bugs. Builds unmodified on both the E1M EVK (Alif Ensemble) and the E1M-X EVK (Renesas RZ/V2N).

templates.title: Peripheral -- GPIO Button + LED
templates.description: Per-peripheral GPIO example via the <alp/blocks/button_led.h> block: reads a debounced button input and drives an LED output. Button is the EVK's encoder push switch, LED is the RGB-red channel's pad claimed as a plain GPIO. Builds on both EVKs with per-family pin macros.

routes.doc: Rotary encoder push switch (PEC12R-4222F-S0024), 10k pull-up + 0.1uF debounce
RGB LED red -- the PWM3 pad as a digital GPIO; the app opens it by the EVK_PIN_LED_RED macro

templates.title: Sensor -- I2C Master (TMP112)
templates.description: Discrete I2C master that reads the on-module TMP112 temperature sensor at a known 7-bit address via the real <alp/chips/tmp112.h> chip driver, contrasted with the address-discovery i2c-scanner example. TMP112 is populated across the AEN, V2N, and V2N-M1 families, so this is the canonical single-sensor starting point.

templates.title: Multicore -- RPMsg (AEN A32 <-> M55-HP)
templates.description: Heterogeneous-compute demo on the Alif Ensemble E8: a Yocto Linux consumer on the A32 cluster and a Zephyr producer on M55-HP (reading the EVK's BMP581 + BMI323 sensors) exchange messages via <alp/rpc.h> over an RPMsg channel, in a cross-core shared-memory carve-out the orchestrator allocates from the SoM's memory map. M55-HE is left at the SoM topology default, keeping the peer-core boot path intact.

templates.note: test_backend includes yocto_host for the a32_cluster Linux slice (linux/), which is real but not covered by the Zephyr twister gate check_template_catalog.py runs against; only the m55_hp Zephyr slice's native_sim scenario is twister-verified.

templates.title: Gateway -- Modbus Server
templates.description: Industrial protocol-gateway teaching example for the ADR 0018 curated `modbus` library: a Zephyr Modbus client and server run in the same process over the transport-agnostic RAW_ADU backend, exercising the coil / discrete-input / input-register / holding-register model used by PLCs, drives, meters, and gateways -- with no UART, RS-485 transceiver, or TCP/IP stack required for the native_sim teaching path.

templates.title: Edge AI -- Cold-Chain Monitor
templates.description: Predictive-maintenance edge-AI starting point: a low-power M55 node reads the on-board BME280 T/RH/P sensor on a slow logging cadence via the real <alp/chips/bme280.h> driver, computes cold-chain metrics (MKT, dewpoint, excursion time) through <alp/inference.h> + the tflite_micro library, and classifies a 4-state integrity result. Same source targets the V2N DEEPX path when som.sku is flipped.
